posts.text: When I finally found the words to tell my parents about what my older cousin had been doing, I expected a shield. Instead, I got a gag order. I was sixteen, and the first thing my mother said wasn't 'Are you okay?' but 'Think of what this would do to your aunt.' For the next seven years, I became an actress in my own life. My family would greet him with hugs while I stood in the corner, my stomach in knots. If I tried to avoid him, my father would whisper, 'Don't make a scene; keep the peace.' The message was clear: The reputation of the family was worth more than my soul.

When I finally found the words to tell my parents about what my older cousin had been doing, I expected a shield. Instead, I got a gag order. I was sixteen, and the first thing my mother said wasn't 'Are you okay?' but 'Think of what this would do to your aunt.' For the next seven years, I became an actress in my own life. My family would greet him with hugs while I stood in the corner, my stomach in knots. If I tried to avoid him, my father would whisper, 'Don't make a scene; keep the peace.' The message was clear: The reputation of the family was worth more than my soul.

When I told my family what my uncle did to me they asked me not to tell anyone outside the house.  I was 15.  They said it would shame the family. At one point they didn’t even believe me.They still invite him to every function and I am expected to sit at the same table and smile. I have been doing it for eight years. I am so tired of protecting people who never protected me. I feel very alone and unsafe. This has caused a lot of trust issues even in my other relationships.
If I couldn’t even count on my family , who can i count on.

Something happened to me when I was 17. I never spoke about it because the person who did it was someone my family trusted. For years I thought it was my fault because of how I was dressed that day. I am 24 now and I still think about it every day. I don't know if I will ever tell anyone in real life but writing this feels like something.

I reported what happened to me to HR at my office. They asked me what I was wearing. They asked me if I had been drinking. They asked me if maybe I had misunderstood. Two weeks later I was moved to a different team. He stayed. I left the job six months after that. Nobody ever followed up with me.

I was harassed by a colleague at work for months. Every time I tried to tell someone they said I was overreacting or that he was just being friendly. I stopped going to work functions. I stopped speaking up in meetings. I made myself smaller and smaller until I almost disappeared. I am writing this because I want someone to know it happened. It was not friendly. It was not okay.

I have been in a relationship for three years where my partner controls everything. My phone, my money, who I can see. I know in my heart it is not right but everyone around me thinks he is so caring and protective. I feel stupid for not being able to leave. I am writing here because I cannot say this out loud to anyone I know.
i was harassed on my way to school yesterday. i dont know who to talk to oabout this

When I told my family what my uncle did to me they asked me not to tell anyone outside the house. They said it would shame the family. I was 15. They still invite him to every function and I am expected to sit at the same table and smile. I have been doing it for eight years. I am so tired of protecting people who never protected me.

I stopped speaking up in meetings. I made myself smaller and smaller until I almost disappeared. I am writing this because I want someone to know it happened. It was not friendly. It was not okay.

My husband hits me when he drinks. I have never told anyone because his family is very respected in our community and I know no one will believe me. My own mother told me to be patient and not break the family. I feel so alone. I found this website and just wanted to write it somewhere that someone might read it and know.

groups.name: Healing Hearts
groups.description: A support group for those dealing with loss and grief.

groups.name: Calm in Chaos
groups.description: Daily mindfulness and anxiety management techniques.
